game_description: Cooperate to cure four diseases before they overwhelm the world.

strategies.strategy_detailed: Always include a Medic or Scientist in your team for faster curing. Pair with an Operations Expert or Dispatcher for mobility. Four-player games can afford a Quarantine Specialist to lock down hotspots.

The starting research station is placed at Atlanta. Identify which cities cluster near high-infection-rate cards early. Plan your second station for the densest cluster — ideally where 3+ cities can be reached within 2 moves.

The 9 initial infection cards define your first crisis zone. Cities infected with 3 cubes on turn 1 are outbreak threats — prioritize getting a Medic or Scientist into those regions within the first 2 rounds.
strategy_stealth: Medic in crisis zone by round 2

strategies.condition: Deciding which disease to cure first
strategies.strategy_detailed: Cure the disease with the most cards already in hand first. Then pivot to the disease spreading fastest (most cards in the infection discard). Never stockpile cards hoping for a perfect hand — partial cures fall behind the spread rate.
strategy_stealth: Cure what you have cards for

strategies.strategy_detailed: Both players must be in the same city to share — no exceptions. Plan routes so the passer ends their turn where the receiver will be. The Researcher's edge isn't location-free trading; it's that she can give any card from her hand (not just matching her city). Still requires same-city presence.
strategy_stealth: Both players same city — no exceptions
Researcher: any card, still same city

strategies.strategy_detailed: Remove cubes from a 3-cube city before the next epidemic card flips it. One outbreak chains to neighbors — if neighbors also have 2 cubes, a chain reaction can jump the outbreak track 3-4 spaces in one turn.

strategies.condition: Outbreak track at 4, chain risk high
strategies.strategy_detailed: Stop all curing activity and commit every action to cube removal. One more chain outbreak may be unrecoverable. Split the team: one player clears the hotspot, another blocks the chain nodes. Curing can wait one round.

Place your second research station where the team converges most often for curing. Ideally a city that appears on multiple player cards so it's easy to reach. Never use an Operations Expert card for a station in a city you'll rarely visit.
strategy_stealth: Don't waste OE card on low-traffic

strategies.strategy_detailed: After an epidemic: the infection discard is shuffled and placed on top. The next 2-3 infection draws will be from previously infected cities. Immediately scan which cities are already at 2-3 cubes — those are imminent outbreak threats.
strategy_stealth: Discard goes to top after epidemic

strategies.strategy_detailed: Split the team geographically but keep one cure closer to completion. Finishing any cure removes an entire threat category — the first cure is a major inflection point. Don't chase 50/50 on two cures and finish neither.

Designate one player as the 'fireman' to handle outbreaks while others push toward a cure. A completed cure means the Medic auto-treats that color — dramatically reducing the fireman's workload in subsequent turns.

Count remaining player deck cards. If fewer cards remain than needed to cure remaining diseases, you must cure on the next available turn or lose. Sacrifice outbreak management temporarily if needed — a cure now beats perfect board state.
strategy_stealth: Cure or lose if deck runs out

strategies.condition: Final disease 4 cards away from cure
strategies.strategy_detailed: Once you're 4 cards away from the last cure, every action that isn't advancing toward it is a risk. Use charter flights, dispatcher moves, and role abilities to compress distance. Win condition is one turn away — play as if every wasted action loses the game.

One more outbreak and the game ends. Every action by every player must either prevent an outbreak or cure a disease. Station the Quarantine Specialist adjacent to the most likely outbreak city. Eradicate a cured disease immediately if possible — it removes the outbreak threat entirely.
